title: Activists Confirm 6,126 Protest Deaths
detail: The US-based Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA) reports that at least 6,126 people have been verified killed in Iran's crackdown, including 5,777 protesters, 214 government forces, 86 children, and 49 civilians. A network of 80+ medical professionals across 12 provinces tells The Guardian the true toll could exceed 30,000. Euronews corroborates with hospital data.

title: Mass Graves and Concealed Burials Exposed
detail: The Guardian reports that the Islamic Republic has been concealing protest deaths through mass graves and coerced night burials. Families are charged up to $6,000 to receive bodies, with security forces monitoring funerals to prevent further unrest. Amnesty International describes the cover-up as a 'heavily militarized clampdown to hide massacres.'

title: US Demands Iran Halt Nuclear Escalations
detail: The State Department issues a forceful statement demanding that Iran halt its 'nuclear escalations, ballistic missile program, and support for terrorist proxies.' The demands come as the USS Lincoln CSG arrives in the region and represent the most explicit US preconditions since Trump's January 23 'armada' announcement.
